title: Portable Bug Packet (portfolio canonical, Bug OS v4)
description: Canonical cross-project bug-packet contract. Lineage: DOCUMENT_OS -> AETHER -> Investment OS -> this portfolio upstream (~/shared/bug-os/). A project copies this file to docs/quality/bug-packet.schema.json and MAY narrow the surface/bug_class enums but MUST NOT weaken the required set, lifecycle, or evidence rules. closure_attestation supports two modes: git-anchored (commit_sha + gate_run_ref; the portable default) and signed-ledger (ledger_event_id + signed + key_id; the strong mode where a signed ledger exists, e.g. AETHER or Genesis post-ATTEST-004).

spec_version.description: Optional Bug OS doctrine version this packet was authored under (e.g. '4.0.0'). Ends silent copy-fork drift: a project can tell which upstream revision it is on.

bug_class.description: Optional routing class. 'ai-behavior' = a defect in agent/model output quality or governance; its regression gate MUST reference an eval case (a directory/marker containing 'ai_eval') — a deterministic test cannot regress a probabilistic behavior. Other classes are advisory routing hints; a project MAY narrow this enum to its real failure history.

root_cause_primitives.description: Which trust-but-verify primitive(s) (a)-(n) (portfolio doctrine, ~/shared/AGENTS.md) and/or Tier-0 runner / lint / semgrep rule name now catch this bug class. The root-cause standard's 'preventing invariant', made machine-traceable.

closure_attestation.description: Binds 'verified' to proof, not a self-asserted flag. Mode 1 (git-anchored, portable default): commit_sha + verified_at + gate_run_ref. Mode 2 (signed-ledger, strong): ledger_event_id + signed + key_id, where a signed run-ledger exists. verified_at is always required; supply the fields for whichever mode the project uses.

gate_run_ref.description: Where the passing gate output is recorded (evidence.md section, CI run URL, or command transcript path)

ledger_event_id.description: Signed-ledger mode: the run-ledger event id that attests closure

signed.description: Signed-ledger mode: whether the closure event carries a valid signature

key_id.description: Signed-ledger mode: the signing key identity that attested closure

audit_event_id.description: Optional audit-plane event id once bug_verified events exist
